title: Visualizing Confidence Limits
description: A diagram illustrating the concept of a confidence interval, its components, and its interpretation for estimating a population parameter.
relationships: A sample statistic (x̄) is calculated from sample data to estimate the unknown population parameter (μ).
A chosen confidence level (e.g., 95%) determines the critical value (e.g., z*) from a probability distribution, which governs the width of the interval.
The Margin of Error (MoE) is calculated as (critical value) * (standard error of the statistic).
The Margin of Error is added to and subtracted from the sample statistic to form the Lower and Upper Confidence Limits.
The resulting interval (Lower Limit, Upper Limit) expresses a range of plausible values for the population parameter, with the stated level of confidence.
